pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly downloads M77789.2 from NCBI E-utilities via wget
Implicitly verified from the task context and UGENE usage
Python execution of Bio.Restriction EcoRI on NCBI M77789.2 sequence. The metadata claim 396 belongs to Addgene/L09137 pUC19 sequences, contradicting M77789.2.
Python execution of Bio.Restriction BamHI on NCBI M77789.2 sequence. The metadata claim 417 belongs to Addgene/L09137, contradicting M77789.2.
Python execution of Bio.Restriction HindIII on NCBI M77789.2 sequence. The metadata claim 447 belongs to Addgene/L09137, contradicting M77789.2.
Extracted directly from verifier.py coordinate tolerance variable

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real biological data representing the pUC19 cloning vector. Specifically, setup_task.sh cleanly downloads the NCBI GenBank M77789.2 sequence. The export scripts strictly verify the output components to the expected paths `/home/ga/UGENE_Data/cloning/results/pUC19_map.png` and `map_details.txt`. Critically, the task metadata (and verifier.py logic) expect the agent to find restriction site coordinates corresponding to the Addgene/L09137 standard mapping (EcoRI: 396, BamHI: 417, HindIII: 447). This constitutes a major bug, as the M77789.2 sequence downloaded during setup has fundamentally different true cut coordinates (EcoRI: 285, BamHI: 264, HindIII: 234).
